one_time_description: Transfer to Minor's Trust in prior year (2021) - large non-recurring transfer of $3,228,636
economic_notes: Nation holds 1/6th ownership in Seven Generations Environmental Services Ltd. (equity method). Ruby Creek Art Gallery is fully consolidated. Nation is part of Sto:lo Nation tribal organization encompassing 11 communities between Langley and Boston Bar.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022. The prior year (2021) had a large non-recurring Transfer to Minor's Trust of $3,228,636 which distorted expenses and caused a deficit of $3,284,794. Current year returns to normal surplus. Accumulated surplus on balance sheet is $12,974,729 including remeasurement gains of $53,103; accumulated operating surplus is $12,921,626. Portfolio investments of $9,177,820 held with Scotia Wealth Management and fixed income instruments. Long-term debt of $41,078 is a non-interest bearing indigenous claims loan from Minister of Crown-Indigenous Relations payable by March 31, 2025. Governance by hereditary process - one Chief and one Councillor. No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $879,108 + Province of BC $97,414 + Stó:lo Xwexwilmexw Treaty Association $86,250 + New Relationship Trust $50,000 + First Nations Education Steering Committee $45,633 + BC First Nations Gaming Revenue $154,390 + Taxation $163,816 = $1,476,611; OSR = $2,493,492 - $1,476,611 = $1,016,881. Note: BC First Nations Gaming Revenue and Taxation classification debatable for OSR calculation.
